title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers slugs, permalinks, redirects, canonicals, and route manifests into a changed collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.
Define Unicode normalization, transliteration or preservation, case, separators, reserved names, empties, length, and collision behavior.
Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Stable Route Identity
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 1579 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Stable Route Identity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public url. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Stable Route Identity, keep Stable Route Identity connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: The page title is the best permanent URL identifier. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Stable Route Identity.

For commit to the mission model, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Stable Route Identity for collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest.
State the Stable Route Identity preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-canonical-origin-758i5o
# Competency: ssg-canonical-origin.
# Case 9058: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_canonical_origin_758i5o(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_758i5o = "758i5o-o5i857-9058"
    assert evidence_variant_758i5o
    evidence_axes_758i5o = (
        "reader758i5o",
        "source9058",
        "parseo5i857",
        "route758i5o9058",
        "artifact9058758i5o",
        "recoveryo5i8579058",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_758i5o)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"
